---
title: "<i>Good Words </i>"
---

## <i>Good Words </i>

**Type:** periodical

### About

Described by CMY as ‘so Scottish and Presbyterian’.  She wrote to Christabel Coleridge (11 August 1871) complaining that ‘respectable people’ could not be expected to read a love story by Matilda Betham-Edwards.  She advised Christabel (1 Jan 1872) to avoid writing for it.  <i>Good Words</i> was published from Jan 1860 to Apr 1911. According to Mrs Gatty it paid much better than the MP: \'Why does an orthodox parson’s wife write for <i>Good</i> Words? When orthodox magazines value her services at £1 per page and copyright reserved--instead of 2s.6d. (<i>Monthly Packet</i> !) --then you may wonder--<i>but not till</i> then.\' (Maxwell, <i>Mrs.Gatty and Mrs.Ewing</i>, 132)

### Letters Mentioning This Organization


---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
